sentences.target: On parle de la nation à table.
sentences.reading: /ɔ̃ paʁl də la nasjɔ̃ a tabl/

translations.fr: On parle de la nation à table.
translations.ipa: /ɔ̃ paʁl də la nasjɔ̃ a tabl/
translations.en: We’re talking about the nation at dinner.
translations.es: Hablamos de la nación en la cena.
translations.de: Wir sprechen beim Abendessen über die Nation.
translations.ko: 저녁 식사 자리에서 나라에 대해 이야기하고 있어요.
translations.zh-Hans: 我们在吃晚饭时谈论国家。
translations.yue: 我哋喺晚飯時講緊國家嘅事。
translations.ru: Мы говорим о стране за ужином.
translations.pt: Falamos sobre a nação no jantar.
translations.he: אנחנו מדברים על האומה בארוחת הערב.
translations.vi: Chúng tôi nói về quốc gia trong bữa tối.
translations.ar: نتحدث عن الأمة على مائدة العشاء.
translations.nl: We praten tijdens het avondeten over de natie.
translations.uk: Ми говоримо про націю за вечерею.
translations.hi: हम रात के खाने के दौरान राष्ट्र के बारे में बात कर रहे हैं.
translations.el: Μιλάμε για το έθνος στο δείπνο.
translations.nb: Vi snakker om nasjonen under middagen.
translations.id: Kami membicarakan bangsa saat makan malam.
translations.sv: Vi pratar om nationen vid middagen.
translations.da: Vi taler om nationen ved aftensmaden.
translations.ja: 夕食の席で国について話しています。

tokens.explanation: Impersonal subject pronoun; triggers 3rd-person singular agreement and refers to people in general or to ‘we’ in everyday speech.
Main verb, present indicative 3rd singular agreeing with ‘On’; denotes the action of speaking.
Preposition introducing the topic/complement (‘de la nation’).
Definite article, feminine singular, determiner of ‘nation’.
Common noun, feminine singular; head of the NP complement of ‘de’.
Preposition introducing the locative PP ‘à table’.
Common noun; object of the preposition ‘à’ in the idiomatic locative ‘à table’ (at the table/at mealtime).

sentences.target: La nation fait face à des défis.
sentences.reading: /la nasjɔ̃ fɛ fas a de defi/

translations.fr: La nation fait face à des défis.
translations.ipa: /la nasjɔ̃ fɛ fas a de defi/
translations.vi: Quốc gia đang đối mặt với những thách thức.
translations.hi: राष्ट्र चुनौतियों का सामना कर रहा है.
translations.ja: 国は課題に直面している。

tokens.explanation: Definite article, feminine singular; determiner of ‘nation’.
Common noun, feminine singular; subject of the clause.
Main verb, present indicative 3rd singular; with ‘face à’ forms the idiom ‘faire face à’ (to face).
Noun used within the fixed expression ‘faire face à’; part of a verbal locution meaning ‘to face/confront’.
Preposition introducing the complement of the idiom (‘à des défis’).
Indefinite plural article; determiner of ‘défis’.
Common noun, plural; complement (object) of the prepositional phrase ‘à des défis’.

sentences.target: Vous représentez bien notre nation aujourd’hui.

translations.fr: Vous représentez bien notre nation aujourd’hui.
translations.en: You represent our nation well today.
translations.es: Hoy representas bien a nuestra nación.
translations.de: Heute vertrittst du unsere Nation gut.
translations.it: Oggi rappresenti bene la nostra nazione.
translations.zh-Hans: 你今天很好地代表了我们的国家。
translations.yue: 你今日好好咁代表我哋嘅國家。
translations.ru: Сегодня ты хорошо представляешь нашу страну.
translations.pt: Hoje você representa bem nossa nação.
translations.he: היום אתה מייצג היטב את האומה שלנו.
translations.vi: Hôm nay bạn đại diện tốt cho quốc gia của chúng ta.
translations.nl: Vandaag vertegenwoordig je onze natie goed.
translations.uk: Сьогодні ти добре представляєш нашу націю.
translations.hi: आज आप हमारे राष्ट्र का अच्छा प्रतिनिधित्व कर रहे हैं.
translations.el: Σήμερα εκπροσωπείς καλά το έθνος μας.
translations.nb: I dag representerer du nasjonen vår godt.
translations.id: Hari ini kamu mewakili bangsa kita dengan baik.
translations.sv: I dag representerar du vår nation väl.
translations.da: I dag repræsenterer du vores nation godt.
translations.ja: 今日はあなたは私たちの国をよく代表しています。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, 2nd person plural (also formal singular); subject of the clause.
Main verb, present indicative 2nd person plural; agrees with ‘Vous’.
Adverb of manner modifying the verb (‘représentez’).
Possessive determiner, 1st person plural; determiner of ‘nation’, singular form.
Common noun, feminine singular; direct object of ‘représentez’.
Temporal adverb specifying time; orthographically contains an apostrophe but functions as a single word.

sentences.target: Je rêve d’une nation plus solidaire.

translations.fr: Je rêve d’une nation plus solidaire.
translations.en: I dream of a more united nation.
translations.es: Sueño con una nación más unida.
translations.de: Ich träume von einer stärker geeinten Nation.
translations.zh-Hans: 我梦想一个更加团结的国家。
translations.yue: 我夢想有一個更加團結嘅國家。
translations.ru: Я мечтаю ο более объединённой нации.
translations.pt: Eu sonho com uma nação mais unida.
translations.he: אני חולם על אומה מאוחדת יותר.
translations.tr: Daha birlik içinde bir ulus hayal ediyorum.
translations.vi: Tôi mơ về một quốc gia đoàn kết hơn.
translations.nl: Ik droom van een meer verenigde natie.
translations.uk: Я мрію про більш згуртовану націю.
translations.hi: मैं एक अधिक एकजुट राष्ट्र का सपना देखता हूँ.
translations.nb: Jeg drømmer om en mer samlet nasjon.
translations.id: Aku bermimpi tentang sebuah bangsa yang lebih bersatu.
translations.sv: Jag drömmer om en mer enad nation.
translations.ro: Visez la o națiune mai unită.
translations.da: Jeg drømmer om en mere samlet nation.
translations.ja: 私はより団結した国を夢見ています。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, 1st person singular; subject of the clause.
Main verb, present indicative 1st singular; governs the prepositional complement with ‘de’.
Preposition ‘de’ elided before a vowel and attached to the article ‘une’; introduces the NP ‘nation’.
Common noun, feminine singular; complement of the preposition ‘de’.
Adverb of degree forming a comparative; modifies the adjective ‘solidaire’.
Adjective describing ‘nation’; agrees in gender/number (form identical in masc./fem. singular).

La nation fait face à des défis.
Vous représentez bien notre nation aujourd’hui.
Je rêve d’une nation plus solidaire.

You represent our nation well today.
I dream of a more united nation.
